title: UK study visa applications plunge 40% in April
summary_en: UK study visa applications this year have dropped by a third compared to 2025, as new Home Office data reveals the fewest April applicants in five years.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: UK study visa applications plunge 40% in April

title: OfS: English universities warned of “over-optimism” as a third face deficits
summary_en: England’s higher education watchdog has revealed over a third of universities faced deficits last year, with the figure set to rise in 2025/26.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: OfS: English universities warned of “over-optimism” as a third face deficits

title: King’s College London and Cranfield announce proposed 2027 merger
summary_en: King’s College London and Cranfield University have announced plans to merge in 2027, with university leaders saying the move would create a globally competitive institution spanning engineering, defence, environmental science and business education.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: King’s College London and Cranfield announce proposed 2027 merger

title: New bipartisan caucus backs US study abroad
summary_en: A bipartisan group of US policymakers has launched a caucus to bolster support for international exchange programs in Congress.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: New bipartisan caucus backs US study abroad

title: UK universities launch China ‘High Achievers Program’ to boost student pathways
summary_en: A group of UK universities has launched a new initiative aimed at strengthening pathways for Chinese students, as institutions look to move beyond competition and towards collaboration in a challenging recruitment market.
author: Amy Baker
title_cn: UK universities launch China ‘High Achievers Program’ to boost student pathways

title: NZ international enrolments hit 92k as recovery gathers pace
summary_en: New Zealand’s international education recovery is accelerating, with enrolments climbing 11% year-on-year to reach 92,580 students in 2025, according to new figures from Education New Zealand (ENZ).
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: NZ international enrolments hit 92k as recovery gathers pace

title: ICE director brands OPT a “magnet for fraud”
summary_en: The head of America’s notorious Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) has called the US post-study work program a “magnet for fraud”, vowing to “relentlessly investigate” students and employers.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: ICE director brands OPT a “magnet for fraud”

title: Revealed: the best-performing agents for New Zealand
summary_en: Immigration New Zealand has published the top-performing agents for nine source countries based on application volume and visa approval rates. The data also reveals a higher visa approval rate for agent-linked applications than in previous years.
author: The PIE News team
title_cn: Revealed: the best-performing agents for New Zealand

title: US phases out international student crisis protections
summary_en: The US government has quietly phased out a policy that provided regulatory exemptions for students from regions marred by war, natural disaster or financial crises.
author: Polly Nash
title_cn: US phases out international student crisis protections

title: MPs warn UK university insolvency risk grows amid international student pressures
summary_en: UK MPs have raised concerns over new visa compliance metrics, the international student levy and lack of government planning for a potential major university collapse.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: MPs warn UK university insolvency risk grows amid international student pressures

title: India seeks database of outbound students amid global conflicts
summary_en: Amid the Russia-Ukraine war and recent Middle East tensions, India’s ministry of external affairs is reportedly planning a dedicated platform for Indians going abroad for education and employment to strengthen emergency coordination.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: India seeks database of outbound students amid global conflicts

title: Global HE enrolment reaches 269 million as student mobility triples
summary_en: UNESCO’s first Higher Education Global Trends Report highlights rapid growth in global participation and student mobility, even as inequalities, affordability pressures and quality concerns persist.
author: Abhishek Nair
title_cn: Global HE enrolment reaches 269 million as student mobility triples

title: Universities urged to “think differently” as half report enrolment declines
summary_en: A survey of 254 universities across 36 countries has found a significant decline in international enrolments, with institutions pointing to restrictive policies and visa issues as key factors.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: Universities urged to “think differently” as half report enrolment declines

title: Canada overhauls immigration consultant regulations
summary_en: The Canadian government has announced new rules for immigration consultants including increased penalties, an expanded public register and compensation for victims of dishonest acts.
author: Polly Nash

title: Emerging Futures 9: visa uncertainty reshapes student demand globally
summary_en: More international students are ruling out study destinations earlier because of visa uncertainty, costs and a lack of clarity about what an application will involve, according to new global research from IDP Education.
author: Kim Martin
title_cn: Emerging Futures 9: visa uncertainty reshapes student demand globally
